It seems like there is an indentation error on line 8. Based on the error message, it looks like there is no indentation on the next line after 'for' statement on line 7. Make sure that there is an indentation of four spaces in each subsequent line after the `for` statement.


how can i make it only download theaudio

To download only the audio of a video, you need to use the `audio_only` attribute of the `streams` object.

Instead of `video.streams.get_highest_resolution()`, you can use `video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()` to get only the audio stream of the video.

Here's the updated code:

```
from pytube import Playlist

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist and download only the audio
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
```

This code will download only the audio of each video in the playlist.
what quality is it downloaded in?

By default, `audio_only` downloads the audio stream with the highest bitrate available. The actual bitrate of the downloaded audio stream may vary from video to video, as it depends on the encoding options chosen by the video uploader.

If you want to verify the bitrate of the downloaded audio, you can print the `abr` attribute of the downloaded stream object. This attribute stands for "average bitrate".

Here's an example:

```
from pytube import Playlist

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist and download only the audio
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    print(f"Downloaded {audio.abr} kbps audio for {video.title}")
```

This code will download the highest bitrate audio stream available for each video in the playlist and print the bitrate of the downloaded audio.
how can i make it so each file does as the thumbnail of the video, you only need to explaint it

To download the thumbnail of a video along with the audio, you can use the `thumbnail_url` attribute of the `Video` object to get the URL of the video's thumbnail image, and then download it separately using a library like `urllib`.

Here's an updated version of the code that downloads both the audio and the thumbnail:

```
import urllib.request
from pytube import Playlist

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")
```

This code downloads the highest bitrate audio stream available for each video in the playlist and saves it to disk, along with the corresponding thumbnail image. The thumbnail image is saved to disk using the video's title as the filename, with a ".jpg" extension.
make it so the name of the channel it was from is written as the contributing artist

To get the name of the channel that a video belongs to, you can use the `author` attribute of the `Video` object.

To write the name of the channel as the contributing artist, you can use a file metadata library like `mutagen` to set the "artist" tag of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel.

Here's an updated version of the code that sets the "artist" tag of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel:

```
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    audio_file = mutagen.File(audio.default_filename)
    audio_file["artist"] = video.author
    audio_file.save()
```

This code downloads the highest bitrate audio stream available for each video in the playlist and saves it to disk, along with the corresponding thumbnail image. The name of the channel that the video belongs to is used as the contributing artist for the downloaded audio file, using the `mutagen` library.

The error message suggests that the downloaded audio file "Hit Em Up.mp4" could not be found.

This might happen for a number of reasons. Perhaps the audio download failed, or the audio file was named something different than what was expected.

To troubleshoot the issue, you can add some print statements to the code to check the filenames and paths of the downloaded files.

```
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    audio_file = mutagen.File(audio.default_filename)
    print(f"Downloaded {audio.default_filename} for {video.title}")
    audio_file["artist"] = video.author
    audio_file.save()
```

This code adds a print statement that shows the filename of the downloaded audio file and the title of the video it was downloaded from.

By examining the output of the print statements, you should be able to determine why the "Hit Em Up.mp4" file could not be found.
it is downloaded but after 1 song the programm stops and shows that errror

It's possible that the error is caused by the attempt to load a non-existent file. This can happen if the program is running too fast and hasn't finished writing the file to disk before attempting to load it.

To avoid this issue, you can add a delay between downloading the audio and loading the file metadata. You can use the `time` module to add a delay between the two operations.

Here's an updated version of the code that waits for a short amount of time after downloading the audio before loading the file metadata:

```
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist
import time

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")
    
    # Wait for a short amount of time to ensure that the file is available
    time.sleep(1)

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    audio_file = mutagen.File(audio.default_filename)
    print(f"Downloaded {audio.default_filename} for {video.title}")
    audio_file["artist"] = video.author
    audio_file.save()
```

In this code, the `time.sleep(1)` statement adds a delay of one second between downloading the audio and loading the file metadata. This should give enough time for the file to finish writing to disk before attempting to load it.

This should prevent the `FileNotFoundError` from occurring in subsequent iterations of the loop.
same result

In that case, it may be necessary to add a longer delay between downloading the audio and loading the file metadata.

You can try increasing the delay to see if that resolves the issue. For example, you can use a delay of five seconds by changing the `sleep` statement to `time.sleep(5)`.

```
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist
import time

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")
    
    # Wait for a longer amount of time to ensure that the file is available
    time.sleep(5)

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    audio_file = mutagen.File(audio.default_filename)
    print(f"Downloaded {audio.default_filename} for {video.title}")
    audio_file["artist"] = video.author
    audio_file.save()
```

Alternatively, you can try using a `while` loop to check if the file exists before attempting to load it. Here's an example:

```
import os
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist
import time

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")
    
    # Wait until the file exists before loading the metadata
    while not os.path.exists(audio.default_filename):
        time.sleep(1)

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    audio_file = mutagen.File(audio.default_filename)
    print(f"Downloaded {audio.default_filename} for {video.title}")
    audio_file["artist"] = video.author
    audio_file.save()
```

In this code, the `while` loop checks if the downloaded file exists before attempting to load the metadata. If the file doesn't exist, it waits for one second before trying again. This ensures that the file is available before trying to load its metadata.
how can i change th file type to mp3

To convert the downloaded audio file to MP3 format, you can use a library like `pydub`.

First, you need to install `pydub` by running `pip install pydub` in your command prompt or terminal.

Here's an updated version of the code that uses `pydub` to convert the downloaded audio file to MP3 format:

```
import urllib.request
import mutagen
from pytube import Playlist
import time
from pydub import AudioSegment

# Create object for the YouTube playlist
pl = Playlist("https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLNFI79D7MYqPlOvLY1TqRKnZWCGKMIIGw")

# Loop through all the videos in the playlist, download only the audio, and save the thumbnail
for video in pl.videos:
    audio = video.streams.filter(only_audio=True).first()
    audio_file_path = audio.download(r"C:\Users\elias\PycharmProjects")
    thumbnail_url = video.thumbnail_url
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(thumbnail_url, f"{video.title}.jpg")
    
    # Wait for a short amount of time to ensure that the file is available
    time.sleep(1)

    # Convert the downloaded audio file to MP3 format
    audio_file = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_file_path)
    mp3_file_path = f"{video.title}.mp3"
    audio_file.export(mp3_file_path, format="mp3")

    # Set the artist metadata of the downloaded audio file to the name of the channel
    mp3_file = mutagen.File(mp3_file_path)
    print(f"Downloaded {mp3_file_path} for {video.title}")
    mp3_file["artist"] = video.author
    mp3_file.save()
```

In this code, the `AudioSegment` class from `pydub` is used to load the downloaded audio file, and the `export` method is used to save the file in MP3 format. The resulting MP3 file is saved to disk with a filename based on the title of the video.

After converting the file to MP3 format, the `mutagen` library is used to set the "artist" metadata tag of the MP3 file.

Note that `pydub` requires `ffmpeg` to be installed on your system in order to work. You can download `ffmpeg` from https://www.ffmpeg.org/download.html and add it to your system's `PATH` environment variable.
